title: V1 pilot droid
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{DroidSeries |image=[[File:V1_pilot_droid.jpg]] |name=V1 pilot droid |creator= |manufacturer=[[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton]]''[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook]]'' |designer= |homeworld= |line=[[V-series pilot droid]]''[[Cynabar's Fantastic Technology: Droids]]'' |model= |class= |degree= |cost=*11,800 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] (new)[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook'', Second Edition]] *9,600 credits (used) |length= |width= |height=1 [[Meter/Legends|meter]] |mass= |gender= |sensor= |plating= |armament= |equipment=*1 retractable arm *3 wheeled legs *Memory for three pre-programmed [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jumps *[[Photoreceptor/Legends|Photoreceptor]] ([[Human/Legends|Human]] range) *[[I/O jack|Starship interface jack]] *[[Viewscreen/Legends|Video display screen]] |affiliation= |firstmade= |retired= }} {{Quote|I thought droids were forbidden to pilot spacecraft. Deep-down programming inhibitions.''"
"''They are, except for Vee Ones and a few special cases.|[[Kell Tainer]] and [[Tyria Sarkin Tainer|Tyria Sarkin]]|''[[X-Wing: Wraith Squadron]]''}} [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton's]] '''V1 pilot droids''' were the first model in their [[V-series pilot droid]] line. Like other [[Pilot droid/Legends|pilot droids]], they were designed to supplement or replace biological [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] for [[Starship/Legends|starships]]. As the most common pilot droid model in use during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], they were used by both the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. ==Characteristics== Unlike the [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series]] [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droids]], which preceded the V1, the design and programming of the V1 droids concentrated less on repair and [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] navigation, and more on acting as automated [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] for short haul transport [[Starship/Legends|spacecraft]]. V1 droids, like the R2-units they resembled, had one-meter tall cylindrical bodies, dome-shaped heads and propelled themselves on three wheeled legs, the middle one being retractable. Internally, V1 droids had a retractable arm and a [[I/O jack|starship interface jack]]. The interface jack was the droid's most important component, which they could use to access almost all the functions of a vessel to replace or supplement a living pilot. In addition to their expert piloting skills, their standard programming included basic skills in repairing space transports, computers, and astrogation. V1 droids could only store three sets of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] coordinates, as opposed to an R2 unit's ten.[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'', Second Edition]] ==History== V1 pilot droids were the most common type of [[Pilot droid/Legends|pilot droid]] in use during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. V1 droids cost 11,800 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] when new, with used models going for 9,600 credits. The high price was a consequence of the new technology used in their production. This made them too expensive for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] to purchase new, though they gladly accepted them when they were donated or stolen from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. V1 units were used by the Alliance to run cargo when living pilots could not be spared. Both Rebel and Imperial V1 droids were programmed to recognize friendly and enemy starships, but they were not meant to be used in combat. When attacked, they could only raise shields and perform evasive maneuvers. Alliance droids were usually fitted with self-destruct mechanisms to prevent the Empire from learning anything from captured droids. V1 droids served as systems operators aboard the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star I]].''[[Death Star Technical Companion]]'' The [[V6-series pilot droid]] was the successor to the V1-series in Industrial Automaton's pilot droid line. ==Appearances== *''[[X-Wing: Wraith Squadron]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Wraith Squadron (unabridged audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *''[[Vision of the Future]]'' {{Ab|Vision of the Future (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} ==Sources== *''[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook]]'' *''[[Death Star Technical Companion]]'' *[[Death Star Technical Companion (Second Edition)|''Death Star Technical Companion'', Second Edition]] *[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook'', Second Edition]] *{{JournalCite|10|Cracken's Rebel Field Guide: Slicing Aids}} *''[[Shadows of the Empire Planets Guide]]'' {{ID|VI pilot droid}} *''[[Cynabar's Fantastic Technology: Droids]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |nl=V1 Pilot Droid }} [[Category:Droid models of the Alliance to Restore the Republic]] [[Category:Droid models of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Industrial Automaton products]] [[Category:Pilot droid models]]
